description: Statically compiled, per-target Oscan runtime archives suitable for linking with native-backend object files. Hosted archives link against libc; freestanding archives have no libc or shared runtime dependency (they still link against small per-target system import libraries at final executable link time, e.g. Win32 kernel32/ws2_32 or nothing at all on Unix).

compiler_validation.cross_target_rule: The configured compiler triple must match {target}. Bare clang may be retargeted only with an explicit target triple and sysroot.
compiler_validation.publication_rule: The manifest is published before its matching archive; the archive rename is the atomic visibility point.

hosted.description: libc-linked Oscan runtime (osc_runtime.c compiled in its default, non-OSC_FREESTANDING mode). Portable to any host with a C toolchain and standard library.

freestanding.description: libc-free Oscan runtime: arena allocator, checked arithmetic, strings, panic handler, plus the OS/graphics/image/SVG/TrueType/TLS feature libraries from deps/laststanding that osc_runtime.c calls into directly in OSC_FREESTANDING mode. Mirrors the freestanding include preamble emitted by src/codegen.rs so its ABI matches what transpiled freestanding programs already produce.

freestanding_core.description: Sibling of 'freestanding' without the graphics/image/SVG/TrueType feature libraries (l_gfx.h/l_img.h/l_svg.h/l_tt.h) — same core runtime, sockets, and TLS. src/backend/link.rs links a program against this smaller archive instead of 'freestanding' whenever the program's own object has no undefined osc_gfx_*/osc_canvas_*/osc_clipboard_*/osc_img_*/osc_svg_*/osc_tt_* symbol, since --gc-sections cannot fully strip the graphics feature libraries' shared floating-point constant pool out of the full archive (see osc_runtime_freestanding_core.c's docs). Same ABI/ordering as 'freestanding' otherwise.

freestanding.note: Windows freestanding TLS uses Schannel (secur32/crypt32) instead of BearSSL, so no embed_bearssl_from entry is needed here. Only -lkernel32 is part of the archive contract. For GNU ld, src/backend/link.rs adds -lws2_32/-luser32/-lgdi32/-lsecur32/-lcrypt32 per program via detect_windows_feature_libs. LLD must see all five while resolving undefined imports in dead runtime sections, then garbage-collects unused import thunks; both paths keep hello_world's final dependency set at KERNEL32.dll only.

freestanding_core.note: Same link-time contract as 'freestanding' (sockets/TLS are unchanged, so the same optional import libraries can be needed); only the source translation unit differs (no gfx/img/svg/tt).
